summary: The average diameter of the apical foramen remains stable between ages 18-25 without notable variation; thus, it can reasonably approximate using commonly cited figures such as those provided by option B at around 502 micrometers. This stability supports selecting middle-range estimates when specific data are lacking or variable. To maintain consistency with previous findings and practical application needs, option B appears well-supported clinically despite not being explicitly stated elsewhere directly related here today’s
